name: Iran

visa_free.notes: Bilateral visa waiver agreement. Ordinary passport holders may enter visa-free.
Bilateral visa waiver agreement.

Under Memorandum of Understanding between Iran and Armenia (in force 10 August 2016), nationals of both countries may enter, transit, exit and stay without visa for maximum 90 days within any 180-day period.
Bilateral visa waiver agreement. Ordinary passport holders. Multiple 2024-2025 sources confirm 14 days (corrected from earlier record of 15 days).
Bilateral visa waiver agreement. Entry by direct flights from Damascus only. 90-day stay confirmed by multiple 2025 sources.
Bilateral visa waiver agreement.
Bilateral visa waiver agreement. Ordinary passport holders. Also applies to Hong Kong SAR and Macao SAR passport holders (21 days).

Bilateral visa waiver agreement. Ordinary passport holders. Max stay 30 days per visit. Note: Iraqi passport holders are excluded from visa on arrival — this bilateral visa-free arrangement is separate. Corrected from null.

Bilateral visa waiver agreement. Ordinary passport holders. 29-day stay per bilateral agreement per 2025 sources. Note: the February 2024 28-country waiver also listed Oman at 15 days/tourism; the bilateral agreement with its 29-day term supersedes for ordinary passport holders.
Bilateral visa waiver agreement. Ordinary passport holders. Max stay 14 days per visit confirmed by 2024-2025 sources. Corrected from null.

Bilateral visa waiver agreement. Ordinary passport holders. 29-day visa-free stay confirmed by 2025 sources. Reclassified from visa_on_arrival to visa_free; reclassification reflects bilateral agreement (not a VOA fee-based entry).
Bilateral visa waiver agreement. Ordinary passport holders. 30-day stay confirmed by 2025 sources. Added — missing from prior record.
Bilateral visa waiver agreement. Ordinary passport holders. Updated in September 2025 from 14 days to 30 days (with possible extension up to 60 days). Corrected from null-duration.
Bilateral visa-free agreement effective February 3, 2025. Air travel only (Tehran–Dushanbe route). Maximum stay 30 days within any 90-day period. Added — new bilateral agreement not in prior record.

visa_on_arrival.notes: Visitors holding valid ordinary passports wishing to visit Iran's mainland are eligible for a 45-day tourist visa on arrival at major international airports. Applicants must submit an electronic application at least two days before travel and present a grant notice at the visa-on-arrival counter. Nationalities excluded from visa on arrival: Canada, UK, USA, Afghanistan, Nepal, Bangladesh, Iraq (already visa-free), Jordan, Pakistan, Somalia, Sri Lanka, Israel, and Colombia.

e_visa.notes: Iran offers an e-visa (electronic authorization code) via evisatraveller.mfa.ir. As of 2025, citizens of approximately 48 countries are eligible. E-visa is valid for 90 days from date of issue, allows a stay of up to 30 days. Citizens of USA, UK, Canada, and Colombia are not eligible for e-visa and must apply at an Iranian embassy or consulate. Processing time is within 10 business days via official portal; authorized travel agencies may process in 4-7 business days with service fees.

visa_policy.notes: Iran's visa policy underwent significant changes in 2024-2025. The February 2024 28-country visa waiver grants 15-day tourism-only stays (once per 6 months, non-extendable). Older bilateral agreements typically grant longer stays (Turkey 90 days, Armenia 90 days, Georgia 45 days, etc.). Key 2025 updates: (1) India's visa-free access was SUSPENDED effective November 22, 2025, due to fraud/trafficking concerns; (2) Iran–Tajikistan visa-free agreement for air travelers took effect February 3, 2025 (30 days within 90-day period); (3) Iran–Malaysia bilateral agreement updated September 2025, extending stay from 14 to 30 days; (4) In August 2025 a mandatory guided-tour requirement was imposed for European, Australian, New Zealand, US, UK, and Canadian visitors — this was partially relaxed in October 2025, though US/UK/Canadian nationals must still travel with pre-arranged tour packages and licensed escort guides. Citizens of USA, UK, Canada, and Colombia are excluded from both visa on arrival and e-visa and must apply at an Iranian embassy.

conditional_access.conditions: Diplomatic, service, and official passport holders from most countries benefit from additional visa suppression agreements beyond what ordinary passport holders receive. Specific duration depends on bilateral agreement.

India's visa-free entry suspended effective November 22, 2025 due to fraud, human trafficking, and kidnapping incidents. Indian nationals now require a visa obtained in advance from an Iranian embassy or consulate. Iran's official MFA e-visa portal (evisa.mfa.ir) lists 27 countries eligible for the 15-day tourism visa waiver and India is not among them. Iranian embassy in New Delhi officially announced via X that ordinary Indian passport holders must obtain a visa to enter or transit Iran.

cbi.notes: Iran does not offer a formal citizenship-by-investment program. Iran does not generally permit dual citizenship. The investment residence permit (5-year) provides residency, not citizenship. No official CBI program was found on any Iranian government website.

programs.name: Five-Year Residence Permit for Foreign Investors
programs.notes: Iran offers a 5-year renewable residence permit for foreign investors. Originally introduced 30 June 2019 with a minimum investment of 250,000 EUR (or equivalent). On 5 April 2022, the minimum investment threshold was reduced to approximately $90,000 USD. Eligible investment forms include: long-term deposits in Iranian banks and credit institutions, investment bonds and securities, and housing/real estate investment. The permit is administered by the Organization for Investment, Economic and Technical Assistance of Iran (OIETA / investiniran.ir). Executives and foreign technicians employed by overseas firms, as well as their spouses and children, may also qualify. This provides residency only, not citizenship. No official path to permanent residence or citizenship through this program is documented on official sources. Note: some sources cite €90,000 — the ambiguity stems from exchange-rate equivalence at the time of the April 2022 announcement; USD 90,000 is the most commonly cited figure in English-language official-adjacent sources.

data_quality.validation_notes: Validated June 2026. Changes made vs prior record: (1) Azerbaijan max_stay_days corrected 15→14 days per 2024-2025 sources; (2) Syria max_stay_days filled null→90 days (direct flight from Damascus only); (3) Iraq max_stay_days filled null→30 days; (4) Oman max_stay_days corrected 15→29 days (bilateral agreement term per 2025 sources); (5) Kazakhstan max_stay_days filled null→14 days; (6) Lebanon reclassified from visa_on_arrival to visa_free with max_stay_days filled null→29 days; (7) Bolivia (BOL, 30 days) added — was absent from prior record; (8) Tajikistan (TJK, 30 days, air only, effective Feb 2025) added — new bilateral agreement; (9) Malaysia max_stay_days corrected null→30 days (updated Sept 2025 from 14 days); (10) India REMOVED from visa_free — visa-free access suspended effective November 22, 2025; added to conditional_access with basis=special_status, level=visa_required; (11) Venezuela reclassified from visa_on_arrival to visa_free at 15 days (bilateral arrangement confirmed); (12) Lebanon removed from visa_on_arrival section (reclassified to visa_free); (13) China notes updated to clarify HK/Macao SAR also covered at 21 days; (14) source_urls expanded with Xinhua confirmation of 28-country list, Tehran Times for Tajikistan agreement, iranstravel.com and iransafar.co for 2025 updates. [2026-06-14] India IND re-verified: visa_required confirmed — Iran MFA official portal (evisa.mfa.ir) lists 27 countries for 15-day tourism waiver and India is absent; Iranian embassy in New Delhi officially announced suspension effective 2025-11-22 (cited by PressTV/presstv.ir and MEA India); conditional_access source_url updated to official evisa.mfa.ir (source_official set true); airline aggregator claim of visa-free/VOA for India is FALSE.
gaps: Exact list of all e-visa eligible nationalities (~48 countries) not enumerated from official source — the evisatraveller.mfa.ir pages returned redirect errors during this validation.

Official direct-fetch from evisa.mfa.ir, evisa.mfa.ir/en/visa-arrival/, and en-economic.mfa.ir was blocked by redirect loops — data validated via secondary official-domain consulate pages and corroborated travel sources.

The official investiniran.ir investment residence permit page returned connection errors; $90,000 USD figure confirmed by multiple aligned sources but original currency (EUR vs USD) is ambiguous in the April 2022 announcement.
Tajikistan bilateral agreement cited from Tehran Times (state media) not a directly fetched MFA circular — primary official government source could not be reached via WebFetch.

India suspension of visa-free access confirmed by multiple credible news sources citing MEA and Iranian government communications, but the exact official Iranian MFA announcement URL was not directly fetched.

Oman's stay duration (29 days vs 15 days under the 2024 waiver) presents apparent inconsistency — the 29-day figure reflects the bilateral agreement while 15 days may apply only to the 2024 waiver conditions; the definitive Iranian MFA official page could not be directly fetched to confirm which applies.
Countries ineligible for visa on arrival (restricted list) sourced from iransafar.co and traveller guides, not a directly fetched official evisa.mfa.ir page.

The 2025 mandatory tour package requirement for EU/AU/NZ/UK/US/CA nationals was partially relaxed in October 2025; the precise current status (especially for EU/AU/NZ nationals) remains unclear from the available sources.

Hong Kong SAR and Macao SAR passport-holders' 21-day visa-free access is noted in China entry but not recorded as separate entries — should be verified as to whether they use Chinese travel documents or SAR documents.
Russia was mentioned in some older sources as eligible for group-visit visa waivers but was not confirmed from any 2025 source and has been excluded.

outbound_official.note: No official Iranian government outbound visa-list page located. The Iranian Ministry of Foreign Affairs (mfa.gov.ir / mfa.ir) publishes only inbound visa policy for foreigners wishing to visit Iran (evisa.mfa.ir, evisatraveller.mfa.ir). No origin-side page analogous to India's mea.gov.in/vffin — listing countries where Iranian ordinary-passport holders may travel without a prior consular visa — was found on any official Iranian government domain. All mfa.gov.ir and mfa.ir subdomains returned redirect errors during fetch attempts. Rely on destination-side data.
